desires: Immediately stop engaging with the reading response task to preserve my energy for my crew, as I am too stressed to function effectively on this right now.
Force a renegotiation of boundaries with my roommate regarding the room's cleanliness, refusing to let their habits dictate my mood or social availability.

goals.description: Text cohort_3 immediately: 'I'm out, meeting up now. Need to vent about the room smell ASAP.' Leave the library if I haven't already.
goals.rationale: Affiliation is my god. The stress is peaking and I need immediate validation from my inner circle (closeness 7) before I can function. My impulsive nature screams that waiting even five minutes is a disaster. Relationships > Reading Response.

goals.description: Go eat with cohort_3 and spend the whole time complaining about roommate_R's odor instead of studying.
goals.rationale: I need to assert my boundary against this environment by vocalizing it loudly to people who actually care. It feels good to be loud right now and reclaim my energy. If I have to stop studying to do this, so be it; the deadline isn't today.

goals.description: Confront roommate_R about the cleanliness/odor once we are back at the dorm, demanding an immediate change or I'm moving my stuff to the lounge.
goals.rationale: I want to be assertive and set boundaries, but my current focus is entirely on social recharging. I'll handle the confrontation *after* I've eaten and vented with cohort_3. Trying to fight them while stressed and hungry is a bad idea; I need the fuel first.

goals.rationale: My value for relationships (0.5) and need for emotional equilibrium outweighs the task of cleaning or confronting roommate_R right now. Preserving my connection with cohort_3 means avoiding looking unstable, so removing myself is the safest, most direct path.
